sections.title: Listening
sections.description: 15 questions • Approximately 15 minutes • You will hear each recording twice

parts.title: Part 1: Numbers and Colors
parts.instructions: Listen and choose the correct picture. You will hear each recording twice.

script.narrator: Hello children! Let's listen carefully. Look at the pictures and choose the correct one. You will hear each sentence twice.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Number one. I see three red apples.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Number two. The cat is under the table.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Number three. My brother has a blue ball.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Number four. There are five yellow birds.

conversation.speaker: female
conversation.text: Number five. The girl is reading a book.

questions.questionText: Listen: I see three red apples.
Listen: The cat is under the table.
Listen: My brother has a blue ball.

parts.title: Part 2: Animals and Actions
parts.instructions: Listen and match the animal with what it is doing.

script.narrator: Now let's match the animals with their actions. Listen carefully.

questions.questionText: Match the animals with their actions.

parts.title: Part 3: Daily Routine
parts.instructions: Listen to Tom talking about his day. Circle yes or no.

script.narrator: Listen to Tom. Is each sentence yes or no?

conversation.speaker: child
conversation.text: Hello! My name is Tom. I am eight years old. I get up at seven o'clock. I eat breakfast at seven thirty. I go to school at eight o'clock. I like my school. I have lunch at twelve o'clock. After school, I play football. I do my homework at five o'clock. I go to bed at nine o'clock.

questions.questionText: Tom gets up at seven o'clock.

passages.title: My Family
passages.content: Hello! My name is Lily. I have a family. My family has four people. My father's name is John. He is tall. He is a teacher. My mother's name is Mary. She is kind. She is a doctor. I have one brother. His name is Ben. Ben is six years old. He likes cars. I am eight years old. I like books. We have a cat. The cat's name is Snow. Snow is white. We love our family.

questions.questionText: How many people are in Lily's family?

passages.title: At the Park
passages.content: Look at the picture. This is a park. The sun is shining. There are trees and flowers. I can see children playing. One boy is riding a bike. Two girls are jumping rope. A man is walking his dog. The dog is brown. There is a bench. An old woman is sitting on the bench. She is reading a book. There are birds in the sky. The birds are flying. I like the park.

questions.questionText: Look at the picture. What is the boy doing?
What is the old woman doing?
How many girls are jumping rope?

passages.title: Animals
passages.content: Animals are interesting. Dogs can run fast. Cats can climb trees. Birds can fly high. Fish can swim in water. Elephants are big. Mice are small. Lions are strong. Rabbits are soft. Monkeys are funny. Penguins cannot fly but they can swim. Bears sleep in winter. We should be kind to animals.

questions.questionText: We should be kind to animals.

tasks.title: Task 1: Copy the sentences
tasks.instructions: Copy these sentences neatly. Be careful with capital letters and full stops.

tasks.title: Task 2: Write about yourself
tasks.instructions: Answer these questions in full sentences.
questions: What do you like to do?

parts.title: Part 1: About You
parts.instructions: The teacher will ask you some questions about yourself.
sampleQuestions: Do you have brothers or sisters?

parts.title: Part 2: Talk about a picture
parts.instructions: Look at this picture. Tell me what you see.
parts.pictureDescription: A picture of a family having a picnic in a park. There are parents, two children, a dog, food on a blanket, and trees.
prompts: What can you see in the picture?

parts.title: Part 3: Simple Questions
parts.instructions: The teacher will ask you some more questions.
sampleQuestions: What games do you like to play?
What is your favorite subject in school?
What do you do on weekends?
Can you tell me about your best friend?

metadata.author: IELTS for Kids
metadata.notes: Beginner level IELTS-style test for children aged 6-10. This test uses simple vocabulary, familiar topics, and basic sentence structures appropriate for A1 level English learners.
metadata.scoring: Star-based scoring (1-5 stars) instead of band scores
